title: SWE-bench and Code Agent Evals
concept: Understanding how SWE-bench tests agents on real GitHub issues by checking if they resolve unit tests.

exercises.prompt: What does SWE-bench help evaluate in AI agents?

options.text: Code solutions for real GitHub issues

exercises.explain: SWE-bench is designed specifically to evaluate how well AI agents can solve code-related problems on platforms like GitHub.

cards.title: Introducing SWE-bench
cards.body: SWE-bench is a benchmark tool that helps test AI agents on their ability to solve real-world coding problems.

cards.title: What are Agent Evaluations?
cards.body: Agent evaluations measure how well AI agents perform specific programming tasks, like resolving unit tests in real GitHub issues.

cards.title: Think of it Like a Quiz
cards.body: Just as students take quizzes to show their understanding, AI agents use SWE-bench to demonstrate their coding abilities.

cards.title: Example of SWE-bench in Action
cards.body: Suppose there's a GitHub issue that asks for a bug fix in a program. SWE-bench tests whether the AI can apply the right code to fix it.

cards.title: Myth: AI agents only work in controlled environments
cards.body: Actually, AI agents can be tested in real-world scenarios, including challenges they might face on GitHub.

cards.title: SWE-bench Overview
cards.body: SWE-bench evaluates agents on GitHub issues by checking specific coding tasks.

exercises.prompt: What is a key task that SWE-bench checks for in AI agents?
exercises.explain: SWE-bench specifically checks whether AI agents can resolve unit tests, which are critical for maintaining code quality.

exercises.prompt: SWE-bench tests AI agents only in theoretical situations.
exercises.explain: SWE-bench tests AI agents in real-world scenarios, including GitHub issues, making sure they can handle actual coding challenges.

exercises.prompt: SWE-bench evaluates agents on their ability to resolve _______ in real GitHub issues.
exercises.sentence: SWE-bench evaluates agents on their ability to resolve unit tests in real GitHub issues.
exercises.explain: Agents need to resolve unit tests to prove they can handle real coding challenges.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following best describes SWE-bench?

options.text: A tool for creating coding projects
A benchmark for testing how agents resolve code issues

exercises.explain: SWE-bench is specifically designed to evaluate agents on how they resolve code-related issues.

exercises.prompt: Why is it important for AI agents to perform well on SWE-bench tests?

options.text: It shows they can create new programming languages
It ensures agents can solve practical coding problems effectively
It proves they can design beautiful applications
It guarantees they will always be accurate when coding

exercises.explain: Performing well on SWE-bench tests ensures that AI agents can solve practical coding problems, which is crucial in real-world applications.
